text.title: Spring Boot Layered Architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real Spring Boot 3 codebases. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint returns Hibernate proxy fields or throws `LazyInitializationException`; agent papers over with `@JsonIgnore` on the entity.
antipattern.root-cause: Controller returns entity instead of DTO.
antipattern.fix: Define a Java record DTO; map via MapStruct in the service layer; remove `@JsonIgnore` from the entity.

antipattern.symptom: Multi-step write succeeds partially after a runtime exception; downstream invariants broken; database in inconsistent state.
antipattern.root-cause: Service write method missing `@Transactional`; each JDBC call auto-commits.
antipattern.fix: Annotate every write method `@Transactional`. Add a class-level `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` default; let writes override.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint times out as table grows; profile shows full-table scan returning 5M rows.
antipattern.fix: Accept `Pageable` and return `Page<DTO>`; default size = 20. Reject list endpoints without `Pageable` in review.

antipattern.symptom: Mapper interface compiles but throws `InstantiationException` at runtime: `cannot instantiate interface UserMapper`.
antipattern.root-cause: `mapstruct-processor` annotation processor missing from `pom.xml` / `build.gradle`; no impl generated.
antipattern.fix: Configure the annotation processor explicitly; verify `target/generated-sources/annotations/.../UserMapperImpl.java` is produced.

antipattern.symptom: Boot 3 codebase compiles locally but throws `NoSuchMethodError` in CI: a transitive dep still uses `javax.persistence.Entity`.
antipattern.root-cause: Imports drifted; some files still on `javax.*` after the Jakarta migration.
antipattern.fix: Run `grep -r 'javax\.persistence\|javax\.validation' src/` in CI; fail the build on any hit. Migrate all imports.

antipattern.symptom: `@Transactional` method called from another method on `this`; transaction never starts; rollback never happens.
antipattern.root-cause: Self-invocation bypasses the proxy that wraps `@Transactional`.
antipattern.fix: Move the `@Transactional` method to a separate bean and inject it; or use `AopContext.currentProxy()` (with `exposeProxy=true`) as a last resort.
